title: Draft Sections 3-5 of Caves
subtitle: Project: short story called Caves

jobs.title: Write the lure
jobs.subtext: The moment that pulls the character toward the cave mouth.

jobs.title: Write the descent
jobs.subtext: Down and in — the light changing, the sound changing.

jobs.title: Write the blackout
jobs.subtext: The point where the character loses the light. End the section there.

avoidance_move: Organizing old notes because it feels like preparing.
avoidance_line: I just need to get the outline straight first.
why: This story stays unfinished every time I let organizing stand in for writing — the draft is the only thing that actually moves it.
feeling: Steadier. Like the cave is a real place now, not just an idea.

display_cues.why: Organizing isn't writing. Only the draft moves the story.
display_cues.avoidance: Reorganizing notes will feel like prep. It's the detour.

check_in.title: The knock
check_in.question: Still with it?
check_in.sort_of_response: Close it and return to the next step.
check_in.no_response: Stop. Return to the block now.

exit_prompts: Did you finish the block as planned?
What, if anything, pulled you away from the work?
Where will the next block begin?
